research_findings: The evaluator lane already had config, rubric, and pass gating, but no dedicated artifact that isolates what the evaluator should actually read.
The evaluator brief should be driven by the active goal, matching scope decision, rubric, and compact project context, while excluding committee summaries and debate.
A temp installed repo is the right proof path because the brief must work outside this repo and stay deterministic.
committee_feedback: Product council supports this because it turns the evaluator from a conceptual role into an actionable review step.
Architecture council wants the brief to depend on scope_decision and rubric only, not council summaries.
Operator council wants a readable JSON or terminal brief that can be used in installed target repos as well as this kit repo.
committee_decision: Add render-evaluator-brief.py so the independent evaluator has a concrete, portable input contract.

research_gate.summary: Make the independent evaluator a concrete lane by rendering a portable brief from scope decision and rubric without leaking committee discussion details.

product_council.summary: Approve a narrow evaluator-facing artifact that improves readiness without adding a second decision process.
product_council.decision: Proceed with one brief script and temp-repo validation only.
dissent: Do not let the council schema turn into a transcript archive.

architecture_council.summary: Read the matching scope decision from durable state, the rubric from its configured file, and a compact project context from the latest project-data snapshot.
architecture_council.decision: Keep committee_feedback and councils out of the brief payload entirely.
dissent: Do not force all downstream scripts to migrate in the same iteration.

operator_council.summary: Make the script work in both text and JSON form so operators can inspect it directly or pipe it into later automation.
operator_council.decision: Proceed if the brief stays compact and independent from council discussion details.
dissent: Do not increase operator typing cost more than necessary.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Add a lightweight evaluator brief script driven by scope decision and rubric
scope_decision.why_selected: The evaluator is already a configured gate, but the repo still lacks a concrete artifact that defines the evaluator input contract for the active goal.
scope_in: Render the active goal, scope decision, rubric, evaluator persona, and compact project context.
Keep committee discussion details out of the rendered payload.
Cover the script in validate-kit with a temp installed target repo.
scope_out: Do not add automatic evaluator scoring.
Do not change the evaluator gate itself in this version.
Do not redesign report rendering around the new brief yet.
assumptions: A matching scope_decision already exists before the evaluator brief is rendered.
The latest project-data snapshot is sufficient as evaluator context.
risks: If the brief includes committee discussion, the evaluator lane stops being independent.
If the script depends on repo-specific state beyond project-data and scope_decision, it will be harder to reuse in installed targets.
stop_conditions: Stop if the new script requires evaluator results to exist before the brief can render.
Stop if the brief starts duplicating committee discussion.
dissent: Keep the evaluator brief small; do not turn it into a second report format.
scope_decision.next_action: Validate the brief in a temp target repo and then add it to the documented command surface.

critique: The iteration readiness contract is now concrete enough to authorize later implementation gates.
The design now has enough durable structure to justify a real evaluator gate.
The change is narrowly scoped and closes a real protocol gap without increasing ceremony.
The version gives the evaluator a concrete input artifact without changing the broader workflow.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep missing quality artifacts from crashing the new blocker path.
Ensure the brief requires scope_decision but not evaluator output.

review_state.goal_title: Add a lightweight evaluator brief script driven by scope decision and rubric

results.name: validate-kit

history.goal: Add a real repository validation script and use it as the publish gate
Fix publish ordering so loop state and backlog updates are committed in the same version
Add a reference that defines what high-quality project data means for the loop
Add a structured schema for project data snapshots and evidence
Add a script that collects first-pass project data into a snapshot file
Add a script that scores whether the collected project data is good enough to act on
Integrate data snapshot and quality state into the loop's durable state model
Extend reports to include evidence sources and data quality observations
Improve next-goal selection using data quality gaps and evidence gaps
Align wrappers, protocol, README, and examples around high-quality project data acquisition
Integrate the committee-driven loop model across config, scripts, templates, and references
Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state
Read committee review state back into project data and reports
Add a project installation script for the committee-driven kit
Require recorded review state before reporting and publication
Define lightweight councils, secretariat, and evaluator roster in config
Render council responsibilities and output contracts from config
Add a backward-compatible review-state schema for councils and scope decisions
Teach capture-review.py to persist structured research-gate results
Capture council-specific feedback summaries instead of one flat committee list
Persist selected goal, scope boundaries, assumptions, and required validation as a structured scope decision
Add a lightweight evaluator rubric reference and config hook
Allow capture-review.py to persist evaluator scores and pass-revise-fail outcome
Render structured scope decisions, dissent, and evaluation outcome in reports
Expose structured review-state and evaluator readiness in project-data snapshots
Require a passing evaluator result before implementation begins when configured
Extend validate-kit to exercise the structured committee V2 flow
Rewrite committee guidance around the lighter V2 architecture
Allow capture-review.py to persist escalation status and recommended action
Block goal selection when research or data quality says more context is required
Add a lightweight evaluator brief script driven by scope decision and rubric
